reasoning_trace.signal: No products found. Premium D2C presence absent.
reasoning_trace.proves: No premium demand signal for this style at full price.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has no demand at lower price points.
reasoning_trace.tension: Absent while Myntra and Meesho show strong presence — suggests demand is price-driven, not style-driven.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product found: rank 3, 65% off, 3.2 rating, 2500 reviews, review velocity 180 (adjusted 45). Discount context: Subsidized Liquidation.
reasoning_trace.proves: High rank and review count indicate visibility, but discount is the primary demand driver.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove genuine style preference; demand is heavily price-subsidized.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong presence but deeply discounted — conflicts with any notion of organic demand. Meesho shows volume at low price, reinforcing price-driven narrative.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product: ₹299, 8000+ units sold, 3.6 rating, 120 resellers. Reseller growth not accelerating.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume at low price point in value-fashion segment.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at higher price points or premium positioning.
reasoning_trace.tension: Volume at ₹299 aligns with Myntra's discount-driven demand, but Nykaa's absence suggests no premium appetite.

reasoning_trace.signal: No prior buy history. No internal data available.
reasoning_trace.proves: No ground truth to validate or contradict external signals.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong: demand is entirely price-driven, not style-driven. Myntra's high rank at 65% off (Subsidized Liquidation) confirms discount is the demand. Meesho's ₹299 volume reinforces price sensitivity.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All external sources agree demand exists only at deep discount or low price. No premium signal anywhere.

eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows subsidized liquidation, Meesho shows price-driven volume — demand is entirely price-dependent, not style-driven.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong — demand is price-driven, not style-driven
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not validated at full price.
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank but only at 65% off; discount is the demand.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is there any genuine style preference beyond the discount?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists only when price is heavily discounted or very low.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has standalone appeal at higher price points.

convergences.title: All sources agree demand is price-sensitive
convergences.detail: Nykaa absent (no premium), Myntra only at 65% off, Meesho at ₹299 — all point to price being the primary driver.
convergences.proves: The style does not command premium pricing.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has no demand at moderate discounts (e.g., 30-40%).

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: If sourced at very low cost (₹200-300), there may be volume opportunity in value-fashion channels like Meesho. Myntra's high rank suggests visibility can be achieved with deep discounting.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: No premium demand exists. Myntra's demand is subsidized liquidation — not genuine style preference. Meesho's volume is at rock-bottom price. Any buy above ₹300 carries high risk of dead stock.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Clean volume signal at low price; no noise flags.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: No premium signal and demand is entirely price-driven. Without internal validation or a clean premium signal, capital commitment is unwarranted.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would the style sell at a moderate discount (e.g., 30-40%) without deep liquidation?
watch_triggers: Nykaa picks up the style at full price
Myntra discount drops below 40% with sustained rank
Meesho reseller growth accelerates
Internal POS data becomes available
eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation — discount IS the demand. No genuine style signal.

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand confirmed on Nykaa, but absent on Meesho — style may be niche, not mass

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong presence vs Meesho absent
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price (₹1850, 4.6★)
conflicts.meesho_says: No demand at all (0 products, 0 resellers)
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't scale to value-fashion, or is Meesho simply not the right channel?
conflicts.proves: The style has genuine appeal at premium price points in D2C and moderate appeal in mass retail
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the style can penetrate price-sensitive markets or is limited to premium buyers

convergences.title: Clean pricing signal across Nykaa and Myntra
convergences.detail: Both Nykaa (0% discount) and Myntra (15% discount, Strategic Value Pricing) show demand not driven by deep discounts
convergences.proves: Demand is style-driven, not price-driven, at least in premium and mass retail channels
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this holds at lower price points or in value channels

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: The style shows strong premium demand on Nykaa with full-price sales and high ratings, and moderate clean demand on Myntra at low discount. This suggests a genuine style preference among quality-conscious buyers, with potential for healthy margins.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Complete absence on Meesho indicates no traction in price-sensitive markets, limiting total addressable market. The style may be too niche or premium for broad adoption. Internal POS has no data to validate.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence with full-price products, high ratings, and substantial reviews

myntra.reason: Moderate presence with clean discount context (Strategic Value Pricing), no noise flags

meesho.reason: Absent presence, no data to evaluate

eval-premium-mirage.bet_rationale: Premium demand is confirmed but limited to two channels with no value-market validation. A small trial in premium-focused stores can test real sell-through without overcommitting.
eval-premium-mirage.key_uncertainty: Will the style gain traction in value-fashion if priced lower, or is it inherently premium-only?

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho shows strong price-driven demand, but Nykaa absent and Myntra moderate with discount dependency — demand integrity is unclear.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Meesho strong: premium vs value demand gap

conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at discount, but unclear if style-driven.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at ₹199, reseller growth accelerating.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a value-only trend that won't work at higher price points?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists in price-sensitive channels but not in premium.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether demand is sustainable or purely price-driven.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho low price: demand quality conflict
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate rank at 35% discount, moderate reviews, unclear driver.
conflicts.meesho_says: High volume at ₹199 with no discount mentioned.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Myntra demand real or discount-driven?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists but may be price-sensitive across channels.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether style or price is the primary driver.

convergences.title: Basic cotton kurti has demand across mass channels
convergences.detail: Both Myntra and Meesho show sales for a basic cotton kurti, indicating broad appeal in value segments.
convergences.proves: There is demand for this product category at low price points.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at higher price points or in premium channels.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (52k+ units) with accelerating reseller growth, indicating a potentially scalable trend in price-sensitive markets. The product is a basic cotton kurti, a staple category with broad appeal.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence and Myntra's discount dependency suggest demand may be purely price-driven, not style-driven. The Myntra product has moderate reviews and unclear discount context, raising questions about demand quality. No internal POS data to validate.

myntra.reason: Moderate presence but high discount risk and unclear driver.

meesho.reason: Strong volume, accelerating reseller growth, clean data.

eval-mass-only.bet_rationale: Meesho's strong volume suggests demand in value channels, but Nykaa's absence and Myntra's discount dependency indicate limited premium appeal. A small trial in value-focused stores can validate real demand without overcommitting.
eval-mass-only.key_uncertainty: Is the Meesho demand driven by style preference or just the low price?

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium and mass segments with no discount distortion.

convergences.title: Strong presence across all external sources
convergences.detail: Nykaa, Myntra/Ajio, and Meesho all report strong presence with low discounts and high ratings, indicating genuine demand.
convergences.proves: Demand for golden kurtis is real and spans premium to value segments.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the same style appeals across price points or if different products are involved.

convergences.title: Low discount distortion
convergences.detail: All sources show minimal discounts (Nykaa 5%, Myntra 10%, Meesho likely low). No suspect or liquidation discounts detected.
convergences.proves: Demand is style-driven, not price-driven by heavy markdowns.
convergences.cannot_prove: Future discount behavior or competitive pricing pressure.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand signals across premium (Nykaa), mass retail (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels. Low discounts and high ratings indicate genuine style preference. Meesho's reseller growth suggests expanding reach in tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate against your own store performance. The price band varies widely (₹349 to ₹1450), so the product may need different SKUs for different channels. Monitor whether the trend is seasonal or sustainable.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high rating, sufficient reviews.

myntra.reason: Rank 2, Strategic Value Pricing, high review velocity, no noise flags.

meesho.reason: High units sold, growing reseller base, good rating.

internal.reason: No prior data available; cannot confirm or contradict.

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources show strong, clean demand with low discount distortion. The convergence across premium and mass segments suggests broad appeal. Consider expanding to multiple stores or regions.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will the demand sustain at higher price points (₹1450) or is it primarily a value-driven trend at ₹349?

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra both show clean, style-driven demand for artisan kurtis with no discount distortion. The premium segment validates the aesthetic, and mid-tier confirms it can work at accessible prices.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho is completely absent, meaning no validation in price-sensitive markets. Internal POS has no prior data, so there is no ground truth to confirm. The trend may be limited to premium-conscious buyers.
